\chapter{Overview}
\label{\detokenize{overview:overview}}\label{\detokenize{overview::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
cTreeBalls: Correlation functions computation with Tree/Balls methods

\sphinxAtStartPar
Author: Mario A. Rodriguez\sphinxhyphen{}Meza

\sphinxAtStartPar
For download and information, see \sphinxurl{https://github.com/rodriguezmeza/cTreeBalls}


\section{Introduction}
\label{\detokenize{overview:introduction}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Correlation function computation with Tree/Balls methods (short\sphinxhyphen{}name cBalls) is a C code for computing correlation functions using tree and balls methods. So far can compute 2 points correlation function (2pcf) and 3 points correlation function for scalar fields like weak lensing convergence.


\section{Compiling and getting started}
\label{\detokenize{overview:compiling-and-getting-started}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Download the code by cloning it from \sphinxurl{https://github.com/rodriguezmeza/cTreeBalls}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Dependencies: cBalls optionally needs gsl version 2.7.1 and cfitsio version 4.4.1 installed in your system. Go to its web page \sphinxhref{https://www.gnu.org/software/gsl/}{GSL} for details or ask to your system administrator. Make necessary changes in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Makefile\_machine}} file and look up for \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{GSL}}. Can be switched off: USEGSL = 0 in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Makefile\_settings}}. I/O cfitsio library is set it OFF. Can be set it ON in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{addons/Makefile\_settings}}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Go to the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{cTreeBalls}} directory (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{cd cTreeBalls}}) and compile (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{make clean; make}}). If the first compilation attempt fails, you may need to open the Makefile\_machine file and adapt the name of the compiler (default: gcc), of the optimization flag (default: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\sphinxhyphen{}O4 \sphinxhyphen{}ffast\sphinxhyphen{}math}}) and of the OpenMP flag (default: \sphinxhyphen{}fopenmp; this flag is facultative, you are free to compile without OpenMP if you don\textquotesingle{}t want parallel execution; note that you need the version 4.2 or higher of gcc to be able to compile with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\sphinxhyphen{}fopenmp}}). The code has been tested with gcc version 10 and would be working with version 11, 12. (In particular, for compiling on Mac \textgreater{}= 10.9 despite of the clang incompatibility with OpenMP).

\sphinxAtStartPar
To check that the code runs, type:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{cd} \PYG{n}{tests}
\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{/}\PYG{n}{cballs} \PYG{n}{parameters\PYGZus{}explained}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The parameters\_explained file is a reference input file, containing and explaining the use of all possible input parameters.

\sphinxAtStartPar
By default \sphinxstylestrong{cBalls} reads/writes catalog of points to analyzed files in 4\sphinxhyphen{}column format with x, y, z columns first and then value of the convergence field. It has a two line header:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} nbody NDIM Lx Ly Lz}
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} nbody\PYGZhy{}value NDIM\PYGZhy{}value Lx Ly Lz \PYGZhy{} values}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Try running:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{/}\PYG{n}{cballs} \PYG{n}{nbody}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{6480} \PYG{n}{o}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{points\PYGZus{}on\PYGZus{}sphere} \PYG{n}{testmodel}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{unit}\PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{n}{sphere}\PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{n}{random} \PYG{n}{options}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{stop}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
In the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Output}} directory you will have a file: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{points\_on\_sphere.txt}}. View its contents to see the two lines header and the 4 columns structure of data.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Note: in the above example \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{points\_on\_sphere}} was not given an extension. By default cBalls gives to the output files the extension \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{.txt}}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
On top of that, if you wish to modify the code, you will find comments directly in the files in the folder \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{addons}}, and the modules you may add must go in this folder. See one of the most simple in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{addons/direct\_method}}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
For the moment you may consult man page:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{man} \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{/}\PYG{n}{docs}\PYG{o}{/}\PYG{n}{cballs}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{m}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
or open with a browser the html file: docs/man/cballs.html

\sphinxAtStartPar
See more details about parameters needed by cBalls below ({\hyperref[\detokenize{overview:parameters}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{std,std-ref}{Parameters}}}}).


\section{Configuration}
\label{\detokenize{overview:configuration}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
cBalls can be configured by switching on/off several options. Configuration file is \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Makefile\_setting}}. Search method \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{balls\sphinxhyphen{}omp}} in folder addons/balls\_omp: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Makefile\_settings\_balls\_omp}} has other options that can be switched on/off.


\section{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{overview:parameters}}\label{\detokenize{overview:id1}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The list of available command line parameters can be consulted using the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}help}} flag:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{/}\PYG{n}{cballs} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{n}{help}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
or its short version:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{/}\PYG{n}{cballs} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{n}{h}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\section{Python}
\label{\detokenize{overview:python}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
cBalls python module (cballys) will be installed if you use:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{make} \PYG{n}{clean}\PYG{p}{;} \PYG{n}{make} \PYG{n+nb}{all}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Note: if problems, you can go to python directory and execute:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{python} \PYG{n}{setup}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{py} \PYG{n}{build}
\PYG{n}{python} \PYG{n}{setup}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{py} \PYG{n}{install} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{n}{user}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
To test it go to directory tests and \textquotesingle{}run\textquotesingle{}:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{python} \PYG{n}{test\PYGZus{}cython\PYGZus{}balls}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{py}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Note: this interface in Cython was tested in a python environment with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{python3.7}}.


\section{Plotting utilities}
\label{\detokenize{overview:plotting-utilities}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Several Jupyter notebooks, written by Abraham Arvizu and Eladio Moreno, are available to process cBalls results. They are in the github repository:

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxurl{https://github.com/joar-cafe/CBalls\_plots/tree/main/benchmarks}

\sphinxAtStartPar
or you can find more scripts in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{tests}} folder.

\chapter{Installation}
\label{\detokenize{installation:installation}}\label{\detokenize{installation::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This section describes the installation settings of cBalls:


\section{Makefile}
\label{\detokenize{installation:makefile}}

\section{Makefile settings}
\label{\detokenize{installation:makefile-settings}}

\section{Makefile plataform machine}
\label{\detokenize{installation:makefile-plataform-machine}}
\sphinxstepscope


\chapter{Parameters}
\label{\detokenize{params:parameters}}\label{\detokenize{params::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This section describes the various parameters cBalls needs for controlling
what the searching process do:


\section{Parameters related to the searching method}
\label{\detokenize{params:parameters-related-to-the-searching-method}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{searchMethod}
\sphinxAtStartPar
(str or list) {[}alias: search{]}
The searching method to use. Default is \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{tree\sphinxhyphen{}omp\sphinxhyphen{}sincos}}. Fastest method so far is \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{octree\sphinxhyphen{}ggg\sphinxhyphen{}omp}}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Use it as:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{searchMethod} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{octree}\PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{n}{ggg}\PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{n}{omp}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
In command line version do not use spaces before and after \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{=}} or it won\textquotesingle{}t be parsed correctly. In a parameter file you have more liberty.

\sphinxlineitem{mChebyshev}
\sphinxAtStartPar
(positive int) {[}alias: mcheb{]}
The number of multipoles to compute for a 3pcf computation. Number of them are: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{mChebyshev + 1}}, because it includes the monopole.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Use it as:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{mChebyshev} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{l+m+mi}{7}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Default value is \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{7}}. As multipoles comes from a harmonic expansion, we may be interested in computing the whole 3pcf. This process involves a FFT, therefore give \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{mChebyshev + 1}} as a power of 2.

\end{description}\end{quote}


\section{Parameters to control the I/O file(s)}
\label{\detokenize{params:parameters-to-control-the-i-o-file-s}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{infile}
\sphinxAtStartPar
(str, default="") {[}alias: in{]} File names with points to analyse.

\sphinxlineitem{infileformat}
\sphinxAtStartPar
(str, default=columns\sphinxhyphen{}ascii) {[}alias: infmt{]} Data input files format (columns\sphinxhyphen{}ascii, binary or takahasi)

\sphinxAtStartPar
The input columns for \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{columns\sphinxhyphen{}ascii}} format:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{x}} = x position of a point in the catalog.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{y}} = y position of a point in the catalog.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{z}} = z position of a point in the catalog.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{kappa}} = The kappa value of the point.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}


\section{Parameters to control histograms and their output files}
\label{\detokenize{params:parameters-to-control-histograms-and-their-output-files}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{useLogHist}
\sphinxAtStartPar
(bool, default=true) Which type of binning should be used.

\sphinxlineitem{logHistBinsPD}
\sphinxAtStartPar
(float) The minimum separation to include in the histograms.

\sphinxlineitem{rangeN}
\sphinxAtStartPar
(float) The maximum separation to include in the histograms.

\sphinxlineitem{rminHist}
\sphinxAtStartPar
(float) The minimum separation to include in the histograms.

\sphinxlineitem{sizeHistN}
\sphinxAtStartPar
(int) The number of output bins to use.

\end{description}\end{quote}


\section{Set of parameters needed to construct a test model}
\label{\detokenize{params:set-of-parameters-needed-to-construct-a-test-model}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{seed}
\sphinxAtStartPar
(int, default=123) Random number seed to test run or useful to change a random region in Takahasi simulations.

\sphinxlineitem{testmodel}
\sphinxAtStartPar
(str, default=simple\sphinxhyphen{}cubic\sphinxhyphen{}random) {[}alias: tstmodel{]} Test model name to analyse.

\sphinxlineitem{nbody}
\sphinxAtStartPar
(int, default=16348) Number of points to test.

\sphinxlineitem{lengthBox}
\sphinxAtStartPar
(float, default=10000) {[}alias: lbox{]} Length of the box to test.

\end{description}\end{quote}


\section{Miscellaneous parameters}
\label{\detokenize{params:miscellaneous-parameters}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{script}
\sphinxAtStartPar
(str, default="") Scripts in shell or python that can be run in pre\sphinxhyphen{}processing or post\sphinxhyphen{}processing.

\sphinxlineitem{stepState}
\sphinxAtStartPar
(int, default=10000) number of steps to save a state\sphinxhyphen{}run info (pivot number completed in the log file).

\sphinxlineitem{verbose}
\sphinxAtStartPar
(int, default=1) {[}alias: verb{]} How verbose the code should be during processing.
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
0 = no output unless there is an error

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
1 = output warnings

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
2 = output progress information

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
3 = output extra debugging lines

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{verbose\_log}
\sphinxAtStartPar
(int, default=1) {[}alias: verblog{]} To print messages to a log file \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{cballs.log}} in directory \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{tmp}} under output directory given by the parameter: {\color{red}\bfseries{}\textasciigrave{}\textasciigrave{}}rootDir\textasciigrave{}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Amount of message information is controlled by the int given.

\sphinxlineitem{numberThreads}
\sphinxAtStartPar
(int, default=4) {[}alias: nthreads{]} How many OpenMP threads should be used.

\sphinxAtStartPar
It is needed to switch on OpenMP: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{OPENMPMACHINE = 1}} in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Makefile\_settings}} and recompile cBalls again.

\sphinxlineitem{options}
\sphinxAtStartPar
(str, default="") {[}alias: opt{]} You may give here various code behavior options.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Use it as:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{options} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{str1}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{n}{str2}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{n}{str3}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
where str\# is one of the:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
stop = stop execution before searching process

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
compute\sphinxhyphen{}HistN = compute NN encounters and save histogram in a file

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
and\sphinxhyphen{}CF = if you use \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\sphinxhyphen{}HistN}} then you may compute and save the correlation funcion of NN encounters (the equivalent to the radial distribution function in liquids).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
no\sphinxhyphen{}one\sphinxhyphen{}ball = during the searching process does not use balls criterion to speed up the code

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{note}{Note:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
It is not necessary to specify all the parameters. You need to give only the ones apropriate to the run. The rest of parameters will use their default values if they are OK with you.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
When you specify the root output directory using: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{rootDir}}, and this is a single directory that will be located in the pwd dir, then do not use \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{./}} at the begining of the name or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{/}} at its end.

\end{itemize}
\end{sphinxadmonition}

\sphinxstepscope


\chapter{Catalog files}
\label{\detokenize{catalog_files:catalog-files}}\label{\detokenize{catalog_files::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This section describes the various formats of catalog points you need to compute correlation functions on them.

\sphinxAtStartPar
They are given with the option: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{infileformat}}.


\section{Standard formats}
\label{\detokenize{catalog_files:standard-formats}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{columns\sphinxhyphen{}ascii}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The file(s) with the data to be correlated.

\sphinxlineitem{binary}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The file with data to be correlated in binary format.

\sphinxlineitem{takahashi}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The file with data to be correlated using Takahashi etal simulations format (can be transformed to Healpix format).

\end{description}\end{quote}


\section{Miscellaneous formats}
\label{\detokenize{catalog_files:miscellaneous-formats}}\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{gadget}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Gadget files can be read using this format. Catalog can be only one file or composed of several files.

\sphinxlineitem{multi\sphinxhyphen{}columns\sphinxhyphen{}ascii}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This format is to be able to read any ascci file with values arrage in columns. Positions can be in any order and with/without header. For example, files with halo catalogs from Rockstar can be read.

\sphinxlineitem{fits\sphinxhyphen{}healpix}
\sphinxAtStartPar
to read maps in Healpix format using cfitsio library. This format permits to apply a mask in the same format.

\end{description}\end{quote}

\sphinxstepscope


\chapter{Pre/Post processing}
\label{\detokenize{pre_post_process:pre-post-processing}}\label{\detokenize{pre_post_process::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This section describes the various pre/pos processing for controlling
what the \sphinxtitleref{cballs} before/after main processing:


\section{Pre\sphinxhyphen{}processing}
\label{\detokenize{pre_post_process:pre-processing}}

\section{Post\sphinxhyphen{}processing}
\label{\detokenize{pre_post_process:post-processing}}
\sphinxstepscope


\chapter{Code structure}
\label{\detokenize{code_structure:code-structure}}\label{\detokenize{code_structure::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This page is a developer\sphinxhyphen{}oriented map of the cTreeBalls source tree. It is
intended to answer two questions before editing the code: where does a run move
through the program, and which files own each piece of state.


\section{Runtime flow}
\label{\detokenize{code_structure:runtime-flow}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The command\sphinxhyphen{}line executable follows this high\sphinxhyphen{}level sequence:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{main/main.c}} creates the run state objects and calls \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{StartRun}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source/startrun.c}} reads parameters, checks them, initializes random
generators, prepares output paths, and allocates the run\sphinxhyphen{}owned arrays.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source/cballs.c}} enters \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MainLoop}} and dispatches to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{EvalHist}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source/treeload.c}} builds one tree per selected input catalog with
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MakeTree}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source/search.c}} walks the tree, accepts or rejects node pairs, and
accumulates thread\sphinxhyphen{}local histogram contributions.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source/cballsutils.c}} supplies shared search utilities, histogram
allocation and normalization helpers, geometry transforms, and acceptance
predicates.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source/cballs.c}} writes evaluated histograms through its \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PrintHist*}}
helpers.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source/cballsio.c}} closes files and releases memory in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{EndRun}}.

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The Cython wrapper in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{python/cballys.pyx}} calls the same C stages, but it
splits them into a dependency\sphinxhyphen{}checked \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Run(level={[}...{]})}} workflow so Python
callers can compute and then read arrays back without going through the command
line.


\section{Module map}
\label{\detokenize{code_structure:module-map}}

\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart
\sphinxthistablewithglobalstyle
\centering
\begin{tabular}[t]{\X{28}{100}\X{72}{100}}
\sphinxtoprule
\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Path
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Responsibility
\\
\sphinxmidrule
\sphinxtableatstartofbodyhook
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{main/main.c}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
CLI entry point. Creates \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{struct cmdline\_data}} and
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{struct global\_data}}, records start times, reads the parameter file
argument when \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{GETPARAM}} is disabled, and runs \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{StartRun}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MainLoop}}, then \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{EndRun}}.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{include/globaldefs.h}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Central include file. Pulls in common libraries, declares global tables
such as \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{bodytable}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{roottable}}, and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{nodetablescanlev}}, selects
GSL or Numerical Recipes support, and exposes project\sphinxhyphen{}wide error and
debug macros.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{include/cmdline\_defs.h}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Parameter registry used by the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{getparam}} interface. Add command\sphinxhyphen{}line
parameters here first, then wire them through \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{startrun.c}}.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{include/cmdline\_data.h}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Parsed user options and derived command state. This is the user\sphinxhyphen{}facing
configuration object passed through almost every major routine.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{include/global\_data.h}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Run state that is derived, allocated, or accumulated during execution:
file paths, histogram arrays, tree statistics, timing counters, input
catalog metadata, and memory ownership flags.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{include/datastruc\_defs.h}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Body, cell, and node layout. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{body}} and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{cell}} both embed a common
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{node}} header so tree traversal can work on \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{nodeptr}} values.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{include/datastruc\_hist.h}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Per\sphinxhyphen{}thread and global histogram workspaces used by the search code.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{include/protodefs.h}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Shared function prototypes and add\sphinxhyphen{}on include hook for APIs that need to
be visible across C modules.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source/startrun.c}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Startup lifecycle: command\sphinxhyphen{}line and parameter\sphinxhyphen{}file parsing,
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{StartRun\_Common}}, validation, data loading or test\sphinxhyphen{}data generation,
output setup, and memory allocation.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source/cballsio.c}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Input/output formats, output directory and filename setup, writing
catalogs, and all teardown routines called from C and Cython.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source/testdata.c}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Synthetic catalog generation for simple cubic and unit\sphinxhyphen{}sphere runs.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source/treeload.c}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Octree construction, root\sphinxhyphen{}cell setup, body insertion, cell properties,
tree threading, scan\sphinxhyphen{}level tables, and tree cleanup.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source/search.c}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
The default \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{octree\sphinxhyphen{}sincos\sphinxhyphen{}omp}} search. Owns the OpenMP pivot loop,
tree walk, node summation, and merging of thread\sphinxhyphen{}local histograms.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source/cballsutils.c}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Shared search utilities: histogram initialization, body property
reduction, acceptance/rejection checks, periodic wrapping, coordinate
conversion, and histogram diagnostics.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source/cballsutils\_with\sphinxhyphen{}RADECPOS.c}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
RA/DEC\sphinxhyphen{}position variant of \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{cballsutils.c}}. Keep it in sync with
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{cballsutils.c}} when enabling or maintaining that build path.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source/cballs.c}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Main computation driver and histogram output dispatch. This is where
search\sphinxhyphen{}method selection reaches the concrete search implementation.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{python/cballys.pyx}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Python class wrapper around the C lifecycle. Converts a Python parameter
dictionary into CLASS\sphinxhyphen{}style \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{file\_content}}, calls C modules, exposes
histogram getters, and releases C allocations.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{python/ccballys.pxd}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Cython declarations for the C structs and functions that Python needs.
Only expose fields here when the wrapper really consumes them.
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{addons/}}
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
Optional search methods, input formats, and bundled libraries. Add\sphinxhyphen{}ons
connect through the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{//B socket:}} include points in core files.
\\
\sphinxbottomrule
\end{tabular}
\sphinxtableafterendhook\par
\sphinxattableend\end{savenotes}


\section{Core state model}
\label{\detokenize{code_structure:core-state-model}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Most C routines receive two pointers:
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{struct cmdline\_data *cmd}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Values requested by the user or read from a parameter file. Treat this as
configuration. It contains items such as \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{searchMethod}}, histogram sizes,
input/output names, verbosity, and option strings.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{struct global\_data *gd}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Values computed during the run. Treat this as mutable execution state. It
owns histogram buffers, input catalog metadata, output paths, timing
counters, tree statistics, and allocation flags used during cleanup.

\end{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The large catalog and tree tables are declared as project globals in
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{include/globaldefs.h}}:
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{bodytable{[}MAXITEMS{]}}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Per\sphinxhyphen{}catalog body arrays.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{roottable{[}MAXITEMS{]}}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
One tree root per loaded catalog.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{nodetablescanlev{[}MAXITEMS{]}}} and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{nodetablescanlev\_root{[}MAXITEMS{]}}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Flattened node tables used by scan\sphinxhyphen{}level traversal and diagnostics.

\end{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The body/cell hierarchy is defined in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{include/datastruc\_defs.h}}. A \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{body}}
contains a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{node}} named \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{bodynode}} and a body id. A \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{cell}} contains a
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{node}} named \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{cellnode}}, a size, a linked \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{more}} pointer, and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{NSUB}}
subcells. The macros in that header, for example \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Pos(x)}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Mass(x)}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Radius(x)}}, and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Subp(x)}}, are the intended field access style throughout
the C code.


\section{Tree and search lifecycle}
\label{\detokenize{code_structure:tree-and-search-lifecycle}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MakeTree}} in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source/treeload.c}} starts with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{newtree}} and a root cell,
then loads bodies into subcells using \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{loadbody}} and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{subindex}}. After
insertion, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{hackcellprop}} computes cell\sphinxhyphen{}level properties used by the search,
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{threadtree}} links traversal order, and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{scanLevel}} prepares node tables
for level\sphinxhyphen{}based operations.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{searchcalc\_normal\_sincos}} in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source/search.c}} is the default search path
for \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{searchMethod=tree\sphinxhyphen{}omp\sphinxhyphen{}sincos}}/\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{octree\sphinxhyphen{}sincos\sphinxhyphen{}omp}} style runs. It:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Initializes global and per\sphinxhyphen{}thread histogram workspaces.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Runs an OpenMP loop over pivot bodies from the selected pivot catalog.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Walks the scanning catalog tree with \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{normal\_walktree\_sincos}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Uses acceptance/rejection helpers from \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source/cballsutils.c}} to decide
whether a body/cell or cell/cell pair contributes.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Accumulates 2PCF and, when enabled at compile time, 3PCF Chebyshev/sine/cosine
components.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Merges thread\sphinxhyphen{}local histograms back into \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{gd}}.

\end{enumerate}


\section{Histogram ownership}
\label{\detokenize{code_structure:histogram-ownership}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Histogram buffers are allocated during startup and search initialization, then
freed through the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{EndRun\_FreeMemory\_*}} routines in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source/cballsio.c}}.
The important ownership rule is: if a new allocation is attached to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{cmd}} or
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{gd}}, it needs a matching allocation flag or an existing cleanup stage that
cannot miss it. This is especially important for the Python wrapper, because
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{cballs.struct\_cleanup}} calls the granular C cleanup routines instead of only
exiting the process.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Common histogram outputs include:
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{histNN}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Neighbor count histogram.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{histCF}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Correlation\sphinxhyphen{}function style output derived from counts.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{histXi2pcf}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Two\sphinxhyphen{}point correlation function output.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{histZetaM*}} and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{mhistZetaM*}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Three\sphinxhyphen{}point/Chebyshev multipole outputs, split by sine/cosine component and
multipole index when the corresponding compile\sphinxhyphen{}time options are enabled.

\end{description}


\section{Adding or changing parameters}
\label{\detokenize{code_structure:adding-or-changing-parameters}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
To add a user\sphinxhyphen{}visible parameter:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Add the default, description, and optional short name in
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{include/cmdline\_defs.h}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Add fields to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{struct cmdline\_data}} in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{include/cmdline\_data.h}} if the
value needs to persist.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Read the parameter in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ReadParametersCmdline}} and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ReadParameterFile}} in
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source/startrun.c}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Print it from \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PrintParameterFile}} so generated parameter snapshots remain
reproducible.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Validate derived constraints in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CheckParameters}} when invalid
combinations would otherwise fail later.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
If the parameter is exposed to Python, mirror it in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{python/ccballys.pxd}}
and set or document it in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{python/cballys.pyx}}.

\end{enumerate}


\section{Adding input/output formats}
\label{\detokenize{code_structure:adding-input-output-formats}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Input format names are converted to integer tags by
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{infilefmt\_string\_to\_int}} in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source/cballsio.c}}. Add a new tag in the same
style as the existing formats, implement the parser near the other
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{inputdata\_*}} functions, and add the switch case in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{InputData}} or
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{InputData\_all\_in\_one}}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Output formats follow the matching pattern through
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{outfilefmt\_string\_to\_int}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{outputdata}}, and the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{outputdata\_*}} helpers.
Keep filename construction in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{setFilesDirs}}/\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{setFilesDirs\_log}} so command
line, tests, and Python runs all agree on output paths.


\section{Adding search methods}
\label{\detokenize{code_structure:adding-search-methods}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Search\sphinxhyphen{}method selection happens in two places:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{search\_method\_string\_to\_int}} in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source/startrun.c}} maps user strings
to integer constants.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{EvalHist}} in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source/cballs.c}} switches on \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{gd\sphinxhyphen{}\textgreater{}searchMethod\_int}} and
calls the concrete implementation.

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxAtStartPar
New search methods usually need prototypes in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{include/protodefs.h}}, compile
settings in the Makefiles or add\sphinxhyphen{}on make fragments, and output handling in
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source/cballs.c}} only if they produce different histogram families.


\section{Add\sphinxhyphen{}on sockets}
\label{\detokenize{code_structure:add-on-sockets}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Core files contain \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{//B socket:}} blocks guarded by \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\#ifdef ADDONS}}. These
blocks include generated or configured files such as \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{*\_include.h}} so optional
modules can add fields, prototypes, switch cases, and setup logic without
editing every core file manually. When adding an add\sphinxhyphen{}on, keep its ownership
localized under \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{addons/\textless{}name\textgreater{}/}} and document which socket files it expects to
be copied or generated.


\section{Python wrapper notes}
\label{\detokenize{code_structure:python-wrapper-notes}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The wrapper class \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{cballs}} in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{python/cballys.pyx}} keeps a Python parameter
dictionary in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{self.\_pars}}. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Run}} converts that dictionary into
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{file\_content}}, calls \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{input\_read\_from\_file}}, then advances through
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{StartRun\_Common}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PrintParameterFile}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SetNumberThreads}}, and
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MainLoop}} according to the requested dependency level.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Getter methods such as \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{getrBins}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{getHistNN}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{getHistXi2pcf}}, and
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{getHistZetaMsincos}} copy C arrays into NumPy arrays. They assume the relevant
run stage has already completed and that \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{sizeHistN}} still matches the
allocated histogram dimensions.

\sphinxAtStartPar
When changing C struct fields consumed by Python, update both
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{python/ccballys.pxd}} and the getter or setup code in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{python/cballys.pyx}}.
The wrapper intentionally exposes only a subset of the C structs; avoid adding
fields to the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{.pxd}} unless Python needs direct access.


\section{Generated and vendored code}
\label{\detokenize{code_structure:generated-and-vendored-code}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The repository includes generated Sphinx output under \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{docs/\_build}} and large
vendored libraries under \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{addons/}}. Prefer editing the source \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{.rst}} files,
core \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{source/}} and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{include/}} code, or add\sphinxhyphen{}on source fragments rather than
generated HTML/LaTeX or third\sphinxhyphen{}party library internals.

\sphinxstepscope


\chapter{AddOn\textquotesingle{}s}
\label{\detokenize{addons:addon-s}}\label{\detokenize{addons::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Add\sphinxhyphen{}ons extend cBalls with optional search methods, catalog formats, and
supporting libraries without making every core file permanently depend on those
features. They are enabled at compile time through \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Makefile\_settings}} and the
add\sphinxhyphen{}on make fragments under \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{addons/}}.


\section{Include files}
\label{\detokenize{addons:include-files}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Core headers and modules contain guarded \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{//B socket:}} blocks such as:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+cp}{\PYGZsh{}}\PYG{c+cp}{ifdef ADDONS}
\PYG{c+cp}{\PYGZsh{}}\PYG{c+cp}{include}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{c+cpf}{\PYGZdq{}protodefs\PYGZus{}include.h\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{c+cp}{\PYGZsh{}}\PYG{c+cp}{endif}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
An add\sphinxhyphen{}on uses these include sockets to add prototypes, struct fields,
parameter definitions, switch cases, and implementation hooks. Keep add\sphinxhyphen{}on
specific declarations in the add\sphinxhyphen{}on include files rather than editing unrelated
core code.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Common socket targets include:
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{cmdline\_defs\_include.h}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Adds new command\sphinxhyphen{}line parameters.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{protodefs\_include.h}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Adds prototypes that must be visible across C modules.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{globaldefs\_include\_*.h}} and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{global\_data\_include\_*.h}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Adds globals or run\sphinxhyphen{}state fields required by the add\sphinxhyphen{}on.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{cballs\_include\_*.h}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{startrun\_include\_*.h}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{cballsio\_include\_*.h}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Adds search\sphinxhyphen{}method dispatch, startup, and I/O cases.

\end{description}


\section{Source files}
\label{\detokenize{addons:source-files}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Each add\sphinxhyphen{}on should keep its implementation and build fragment in its own
subdirectory, for example \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{addons/cfitsio}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{addons/gadget\_io}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{addons/kdtree\_box\_omp}}, or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{addons/octree\_box\_omp}}. The add\sphinxhyphen{}on
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Readme.txt}} and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{To\_add\_in\_Makefile\_addons}} files explain which fragments
must be included in the top\sphinxhyphen{}level add\sphinxhyphen{}on build.

\sphinxAtStartPar
When adding a new search add\sphinxhyphen{}on, wire it through the same conceptual path as a
core search method:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Define the command\sphinxhyphen{}line search string and integer method tag.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Add parameter parsing or validation hooks if the method needs extra options.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Add the method\textquotesingle{}s prototype through the prototype socket.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Add the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{EvalHist}} switch case through the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{cballs}} socket.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Make sure any method\sphinxhyphen{}specific allocations are released by an existing or new
cleanup hook.

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxAtStartPar
When adding a new I/O add\sphinxhyphen{}on, add the format string conversion, the parser or
writer implementation, and the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{InputData}}/\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{OutputData}} switch case through
the I/O sockets.

\sphinxstepscope


\chapter{Python interface}
\label{\detokenize{python:python-interface}}\label{\detokenize{python::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The Python extension is implemented by \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{python/cballys.pyx}} and declared to
Cython through \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{python/ccballys.pxd}}. It wraps the same C lifecycle used by the
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{cballs}} executable, but exposes it as a Python class named \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{cballs}}.


\section{Basic lifecycle}
\label{\detokenize{python:basic-lifecycle}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Typical Python use follows this pattern:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{cballys}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{cballs}

\PYG{n}{cb} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{cballs}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{cb}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{set}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{infile}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{catalog.txt}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{infileformat}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{columns\PYGZhy{}ascii}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{rootDir}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Output}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{options}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{compute\PYGZhy{}HistN}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{cb}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{Run}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{rbins} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{cb}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{getrBins}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{xi2} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{cb}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{getHistXi2pcf}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{set}} updates the internal parameter dictionary. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Run}} converts that
dictionary into the C \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{file\_content}} representation, calls the startup
routines, executes the main search, and leaves C\sphinxhyphen{}owned histograms available for
getter methods. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{clean\_all}} releases C allocations and clears the Python
parameter dictionary.


\section{Run levels}
\label{\detokenize{python:run-levels}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Run(level={[}...{]})}} accepts the name of the last C stage the caller needs. The
wrapper expands dependencies automatically. The default level is
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}"MainLoop"{]}}}, which performs a full calculation. Internally the common stage
order is:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{input}} reads Python parameters into C structures.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{StartRun\_Common}} validates parameters, initializes state, loads input
catalogs or test data, and allocates memory.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PrintParameterFile}} writes a reproducible parameter snapshot.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SetNumberThreads}} applies the OpenMP thread count.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MainLoop}} builds trees, runs the selected search, and evaluates
histograms.

\end{enumerate}


\section{Available getters}
\label{\detokenize{python:available-getters}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The wrapper exposes small, NumPy\sphinxhyphen{}oriented getters for the C arrays used most
often from notebooks and scripts:
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{getrBins}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Radial bin centers or edges as prepared by the C run.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{getHistNN}} and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{getHistCF}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Neighbor counts and correlation\sphinxhyphen{}function outputs.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{getHistXi2pcf}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Two\sphinxhyphen{}point correlation function array.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{getHistZetaMsincos(m, type)}} and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{getHistZetaM\_EE(m)}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Two\sphinxhyphen{}dimensional 3PCF/multipole matrices for the requested multipole index.

\end{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{getNThreads}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{getTheta}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{getrsmooth}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{getCPUTime}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{getVersion}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{getRootDir}}, and allocation\sphinxhyphen{}flag getters expose selected scalar state from
the underlying C structs.


\section{Cython interface}
\label{\detokenize{python:cython-interface}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{python/ccballys.pxd}} intentionally declares only the fields and C functions
needed by the wrapper. When a C field is renamed, moved, or newly needed by
Python, update the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{.pxd}} declaration and the matching logic in
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{python/cballys.pyx}} together.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Memory is owned by the C code after \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{StartRun\_Common}}. The wrapper tracks C
allocation flags and calls the granular \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{EndRun\_FreeMemory\_*}} routines from
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{struct\_cleanup}}. New C allocations that should survive long enough for
Python getters must also be reachable by the cleanup path.


\section{See also}
\label{\detokenize{python:see-also}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
For a broader map of the C modules and Python wrapper, see
{\hyperref[\detokenize{code_structure::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Code structure}}}}.

\sphinxstepscope


\chapter{Hands on}
\label{\detokenize{handson:hands-on}}\label{\detokenize{handson::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This section describes a practical guide to use \sphinxstylestrong{cBalls}:

\sphinxAtStartPar
Let us test one of the \sphinxhref{https://arxiv.org/pdf/1706.01472}{Takahashi} realizations. We download the realization using unix \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wget}} command in the terminal:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{wget} \PYG{n}{http}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{o}{/}\PYG{o}{/}\PYG{n}{cosmo}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{phys}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{hirosaki}\PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{n}{u}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{ac}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{jp}\PYG{o}{/}\PYG{n}{takahasi}\PYG{o}{/}\PYG{n}{allsky\PYGZus{}raytracing}\PYG{o}{/}\PYG{n}{sub1}\PYG{o}{/}\PYG{n}{nres12}\PYG{o}{/}\PYG{n}{allskymap\PYGZus{}nres12r000}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{zs9}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{mag}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{dat}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Then we will have in our working directory the file \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{allskymap\_nres12r000.zs9.mag.dat}}, 3 Gb in size with \textasciitilde{}200 million points distributed on the surface of a unit sphere.

\sphinxstepscope


\chapter{2\sphinxhyphen{}point correlation functions}
\label{\detokenize{2pcf:point-correlation-functions}}\label{\detokenize{2pcf::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{cBalls} can compute 2\sphinxhyphen{}point correlations (2pcf) when counts (N) or a scalar field are involved (like convergence K):
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{NN}
\sphinxAtStartPar
It is the normal 2\sphinxhyphen{}point correlation function of number counts (typically
galaxy counts).

\sphinxlineitem{KK}
\sphinxAtStartPar
So far we have consider that the scalar field is the convergence in weak lensing \(\kappa\). Then this gives the 2\sphinxhyphen{}point kappa\sphinxhyphen{}kappa correlation function.

\end{description}\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
N represent simple counting and K represent a real scalar field, like convergence in weak lensing.

\sphinxstepscope


\chapter{3\sphinxhyphen{}point correlation functions}
\label{\detokenize{3pcf:point-correlation-functions}}\label{\detokenize{3pcf::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{cBalls} can also compute 3\sphinxhyphen{}point correlations (3pcf) when counts (N) or a scalar field are involved (like convergence \(\kappa\)). In particular to compute the correlation KKK the numerical code use the harmonic base.


\section{Harmonic base}
\label{\detokenize{3pcf:harmonic-base}}
